# Project Management SaaS Platform Brief

## Project Overview
Build a project management SaaS platform for small to medium-sized teams, focusing on simplicity and collaboration.

## Business Context
- Company: "TeamSync" - B2B SaaS startup
- Target market: Teams of 5-50 users
- Revenue model: Tiered subscription ($10-$50/user/month)
- Competitive landscape: Asana, Trello, Monday.com

## Problem Statement
Market gap:
- Existing tools are too complex for small teams
- High learning curve for non-technical users
- Expensive for small teams
- Poor mobile experience in competitors
- Limited customization options

## Goals
1. Launch MVP with core project management features
2. Achieve 1,000 paying customers in first year
3. Focus on simplicity and ease of use
4. Competitive pricing for small teams
5. Excellent mobile experience

## Key Features Required
- Project boards (Kanban-style)
- Task management with assignments and due dates
- Team collaboration (comments, mentions, file sharing)
- Time tracking
- Gantt charts for project timelines
- Custom workflows and automations
- Integrations (Slack, Google Drive, GitHub)
- Mobile apps (iOS and Android)
- Reporting and analytics
- Template library for common project types

## Technical Considerations
- Web-based platform with responsive design
- Real-time collaboration (WebSockets)
- Multi-tenant architecture
- Role-based access control
- API for third-party integrations
- Data export functionality
- Security compliance (SOC 2 Type II)
- 99.9% uptime SLA

## Constraints
- Budget: $300,000 for development
- Timeline: 8 months to launch
- Team: 6 developers, 2 designers, 1 DevOps
- Must support 10,000+ concurrent users
- < 500ms page load time
- Data residency options (EU, US, APAC)

## Success Metrics
- 1,000 paying customers in first year
- $50K MRR by end of year 1
- 80% customer retention rate
- 4.0+ rating on review platforms
- < 5% support ticket rate

## Risks
- Crowded market with strong competitors
- Customer acquisition cost (CAC) challenges
- Scaling real-time features
- Security and data privacy concerns
- Feature creep vs. simplicity balance
